checklist_summary.note: both FAILs adjudicated as claim-quality pushbacks (VC-3, VC-14)

phase3_findings.description: command-scope matrix row omits -u GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS; that channel outranks GIT_CONFIG_COUNT/KEY_n, so an ambient value flips the row (AC1 not discharged; reintroduces host-dependence)
AC6 comment '(never the host's)' false: GIT_AUTHOR_*/GIT_COMMITTER_* outrank -c config
'runs the whole thing under a HOSTILE outer environment' — fixture setup runs outside the subshell
four unguarded mktemp calls; empty GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL/SYSTEM reads as unset and silently disarms the hostility (fail-open)
temp files not registered with _suite_tmp_file
hostile system config file is inert under NOSYSTEM=1; comment overstates the environment
AC7 asserts committer only; author half unguarded
negative probe conflates any git failure with 'identity unresolved'
no arrival guard: an early subshell abort records zero FAILs and leaves the suite green
GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS never activated by the hostile env, so every row's -u guard is unproven-necessary (detect-all self-certification)
persist now runs only under an identity-RICH env; #441 AC8's original identity-less property lost its guard
no git version floor for GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL/SYSTEM/COUNT (>=2.31/2.32)
_id2's single-token-name constraint undocumented
'isolate EVERY source' absolute; local repo config isolated by the fixture, not the probe
NOSYSTEM 'overrides'/'despite' implies a mechanism git does not offer
AC4 parenthetical is a garden-path sentence
AC9 has no in-tree regression guard; suggests harness-entry normalization of GIT_CONFIG_*/identity vars
five count-locked claims authored by this iteration's own fix delta

fix_decisions.claim_text: the four new temp config files should be registered with the _suite_tmp_file cleanup registry
fix_decisions.evidence: lib/test/run.sh:76-83 scopes the registry explicitly to WHOLE-RUN temps ('every whole-run temp file/dir is registered at its creation site'); these four are block-local, and the file's documented convention for block-local temps is bare allocation + trailing rm -f (dozens of precedents: GS_PROBE, CG_BAD, DEF_CFG, MAXI_CFG, ST_CFG). Registering block-locals contradicts the documented design. The real defect — the unguarded allocation (fail-open) — is fixed under F4.

parking_evidence.basis: the prescription contradicts the registry's documented whole-run scope; the underlying fail-open it cited is fixed separately under F4
parking_evidence.failing_input: an mktemp failure leaving GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL/SYSTEM empty

fix_decisions.claim_text: the negative probe should assert on git's stderr text ('unable to auto-detect' / 'Please tell me who you are') rather than bare exit status
fix_decisions.evidence: Matching git's diagnostic strings is exactly the version-dependent commit-diagnostic coupling issue #575 removed (the issue's Desired Behavior states the negative control must use git's identity-resolution commands INSTEAD of matching version-dependent diagnostics). The four matrix rows are a positive control on the same fixture and same repo path, so a bogus-path/missing-git failure turns them RED first; the probe is not vacuous in situ.

parking_evidence.basis: the prescription reintroduces the version-dependent diagnostic coupling the issue explicitly removed
parking_evidence.failing_input: an unrelated non-zero git exit under the probe's isolated env

fix_decisions.claim_text: no minimum git version is enforced for GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL/GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM (2.32) and GIT_CONFIG_COUNT (2.31)
fix_decisions.evidence: Adding a git version floor changes lib/preflight.sh, outside issue #575's Architecture Alignment ('Keep the correction local to identity-sensitive fixture execution'). Degradation is loud, not silent: on an identity-configured host an old git turns the AC4 probe RED. The false-green case needs old git AND a host with no identity at all.

parking_evidence.basis: a preflight version floor is outside the issue's stated local-to-the-fixture scope, and the degradation is loud on the realistic host
parking_evidence.failing_input: git < 2.31 on a host with no configured identity

fix_decisions.claim_text: AC9 should be enforced by a harness-entry normalization of GIT_CONFIG_*/identity variables, per the issue #533 precedent
fix_decisions.evidence: A suite-wide harness-entry normalization changes behavior for ~165 other git-commit sites, contradicting issue #575's Architecture Alignment ('Keep the correction local to identity-sensitive fixture execution'). AC9 was verified empirically this run instead (see verification_evidence.ac9_hostile_run).

parking_evidence.basis: a suite-wide harness change exceeds the issue's stated local scope; AC9 discharged empirically this run
parking_evidence.failing_input: a future identity-sensitive fixture reintroducing host dependence

fix_decisions.claim_text: register the four mktemp fixtures with a trap 'rm -f ...' EXIT so a later guard's exit 1 does not leak the earlier three
fix_decisions.evidence: lib/test/run.sh:76-83 documents that re-registering a `trap ... EXIT` REPLACES the suite's single _suite_cleanup handler — the exact defect PR #539 found (SKIPS_FILE/IMPL_SKILL_BUNDLE silently un-covered). Adding a local EXIT trap here would disarm whole-run cleanup, a strictly worse leak than the three temp files it saves on a path that only fires when mktemp is already failing.

parking_evidence.basis: the prescribed remedy would replace the suite's single EXIT cleanup handler, the documented PR #539 defect
parking_evidence.failing_input: a mktemp failure on the fourth allocation after three succeeded

test_first_gate.command: cp lib/test/run.sh lib/test/_mut1.sh; sed -i '' '21456d' lib/test/_mut1.sh  # delete the cmd row's -u GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS; bash lib/test/_mut1.sh
test_first_gate.evidence: 9343 passed, 1 failed — the single FAIL was 'tb(#575 AC1): command-scope config row resolves its own identity'. Guard proven non-vacuous. Mutant run on a COPY; copy deleted.

test_first_gate.command: copy with the seed commit's `env -u GIT_AUTHOR_NAME ...` wrapper removed; bash lib/test/_mut2.sh
test_first_gate.evidence: 9343 passed, 1 failed — the single FAIL was 'tb(#575 AC6): the seed commit attributes to its ONE-SHOT identity, not an ambient one'. Guard proven non-vacuous. Mutant run on a COPY; copy deleted.

test_first_gate.decision: empirical precedence probes (pre-fix, scratch repos only)
test_first_gate.command: git var GIT_AUTHOR_IDENT under crafted GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS / GIT_AUTHOR_* environments
test_first_gate.evidence: GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS resolved 'Leak <leak@e.com>' over GIT_CONFIG_COUNT's CmdName (F1 confirmed); GIT_AUTHOR_* resolved 'HostLeak' over `-c user.name=seed` (F2 confirmed); EMAIL fallback correctly blocked by user.useConfigOnly (no gap).

sweep_evidence.note: F1's class (an identity-source channel a row fails to isolate) swept across all four matrix rows and both negative probes: only the command-scope row was missing -u GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS; the other six env invocations already carried it. Comment-claim class (F2/F3/F6/F13/F14/F15/F16) swept across the whole added comment block.

stale_prose_check.outcome: all five count-locked claims were authored by this iteration's own fix delta and were reworded drift-proof (pin-or-don't-write policy); re-run is clean with zero rows

verification_evidence.ac9_hostile_final: 9345 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped (global+system config, GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS, all four identity vars, EMAIL)
verification_evidence.mutation_checks: M1 -u GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS removed -> AC1 cmd row RED; M2 seed env -u removed -> AC6 RED; M3 unbound-var abort -> arrival sentinel RED (12 assertions unexecuted); M4 env shadowed rc127 -> both AC4 probes RED

$EMAIL identity channel neither enumerated nor isolated; only user.useConfigOnly blocked it and nothing asserts that option
phase3_findings.disposition: fixed in-loop (ed109949 + run-b alignment); -u EMAIL proven load-bearing without useConfigOnly

TB_HOSTILE_SYS 'leak canary' cannot fire under any reachable config
phase3_findings.disposition: fixed in-loop (reworded to inert attribution placeholder)

phase3_findings.description: the four mktemp fixtures leak if a LATER allocation guard fires exit 1
no findings at confidence >=80; independently confirmed all seven git-behavior claims and both fixes

shadow.note: All six new shadow findings were fixed or adjudicated within iteration 1 rather than promoted, because each was a one-token or comment-only reconciliation on the same changed surface. The Critical was a direct reviewer disagreement (comment-analyzer vs silent-failure-hunter on GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM bool semantics) resolved by orchestrator measurement, not by vote.
